license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zn4f5gw
title: Protein Estimation by BCA
author: Vidur Sabharwal

steps_raw.instruction: Make the following dilutions of autoclaved dd. H 2 0 in triplicates BSA conc(µg/ml) 0 5 10 15 20 25 Water(µl) 25 22.5 20 17.5 15 12.5 BSA(2mg/ml)(µl) 0 2.5 5 7.5 10 12.5
Dilute sample in the following dilution in triplicates Water(µl) 23 Sample(µl) 2
Total wells(x) = 18(controls) + 3*n(samples) Make final BCA solution for x+1 wells with 200µl per well and 1 part Reagent A and 50 parts Reagent B from The Pierce BCA assay kit.
Take readings immediately after concentration estimation in a spectrophotometer at 562nm. Make sure the OD is not above 1. If so, do not analyze the data
Export the results in Excel, fit the standards to a linear line and calculate the samples concetration. The actual concentration of the sample will be O.D./2*slope(standards)
